name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-ts
description: Instrument applications with Azure Monitor and OpenTelemetry for JavaScript (@azure/monitor-opentelemetry). Use when adding distributed tracing, metrics, and logs to Node.js applications with Application Insights.
capabilities: Configure auto-instrumentation for Node.js applications with Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry
Set up custom tracing with manual span creation and attribute recording
Create custom metrics including counters, histograms, and observable gauges
Configure trace, metric, and log exporters with manual exporter setup
Implement custom span processors for filtering and enrichment
Set up sampling ratios for high-traffic applications
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner analyzed SKILL.md documentation as executable code. The skill provides legitimate Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry instrumentation guidance with standard environment variable configuration and documented network endpoints for telemetry export.
valueStatement: Application teams need observability for production debugging and performance monitoring. This skill provides complete guidance for instrumenting TypeScript and JavaScript applications with Azure Monitor using OpenTelemetry standards.
userTitle: Add Azure Monitor Telemetry to Node.js Apps
limitations: Requires Azure Monitor connection string and Azure subscription
Auto-instrumentation requires Node.js 18.19+ for ESM loader support
Custom span processors must be implemented in TypeScript or JavaScript
Log ingestion requires separate @azure/monitor-ingestion package setup

useCases.title: Production Application Monitoring
useCases.description: Set up comprehensive telemetry for a production Node.js API to track request latencies, error rates, and dependencies.

useCases.title: Microservices Distributed Tracing
useCases.description: Enable end-to-end trace visibility across microservices using OpenTelemetry standard context propagation.

useCases.title: Custom Business Metrics
useCases.description: Track application-specific metrics like user actions, feature usage, and business KPIs alongside standard telemetry.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Auto-Instrumentation Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me add Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry to my Express.js application with default configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Trace Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry with custom sampling, offline storage, and specific instrumentation options for my production app.

promptTemplates.title: Manual Span Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to track a specific operation with custom spans, attributes, and events. Show me how to create manual traces with proper error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Metrics and Processors
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up custom metrics with a meter provider, create a filtering span processor, and configure the log exporter for my application.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced user building complete observability solution

bestPractices: Call useAzureMonitor before importing any other modules to ensure proper auto-instrumentation
Store connection strings in environment variables, never in source code
Implement graceful shutdown with shutdownAzureMonitor to flush pending telemetry
antiPatterns: Do not skip calling shutdownAzureMonitor on application exit - telemetry will be lost
Avoid setting sampling ratio to 0 in production - you will lose all telemetry data
Do not initialize useAzureMonitor multiple times - it causes resource conflicts

faq.question: What is the minimum Node.js version required?
faq.answer: Node.js 18.19.0 or later is required for ESM loader support. For CommonJS, Node.js 14+ is supported.

faq.question: Do I need to install separate exporters?
faq.answer: No, @azure/monitor-opentelemetry includes the exporters. Install @azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter separately only for custom low-level setups.

faq.question: How do I enable ESM loader?
faq.answer: Run your application with node --import @azure/monitor-opentelemetry/loader or add the import to your package.json scripts.

faq.question: Can I filter which traces are sent to Azure Monitor?
faq.answer: Yes, use custom span processors with onEnd to filter or modify spans, or configure the samplingRatio option.

faq.question: How do I add custom dimensions to all telemetry?
faq.answer: Use a custom span processor's onEnd method to add attributes to spans, or use resourceFromAttributes when configuring the SDK.

faq.question: What happens if Azure Monitor is unavailable?
faq.answer: Enable offline storage with the storageDirectory option to buffer telemetry locally and retry later.
